creator: bapt@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Fri, Feb 07, 2014 at 04:13:48PM +0000, O. Hartmann wrote:
> 
> >Number:         186535
> >Category:       ports
> >Synopsis:       textproc/libvisio: configure: error: Required boost headers not found. install boost >= 1.36
> >Confidential:   no
> >Severity:       non-critical
> >Priority:       low
> >Responsible:    freebsd-ports-bugs
> >State:          open
> >Quarter:        
> >Keywords:       
> >Date-Required:
> >Class:          sw-bug
> >Submitter-Id:   current-users
> >Arrival-Date:   Fri Feb 07 16:20:00 UTC 2014
> >Closed-Date:
> >Last-Modified:
> >Originator:     O. Hartmann
> >Release:        FreeBSD 11.0-CURRENT/amd64
> >Organization:
> >Environment:
> FreeBSD 11.0-CURRENT #0 r261490: Tue Feb  4 18:17:50 CET 2014 amd64
> >Description:
> Update of port textproc/libvisio fails with:
> 
> 
> [...]
> 
> configure: error: Required boost headers not found. install boost >= 1.36
> ===>  Script "configure" failed unexpectedly.
> Please report the problem to office@FreeBSD.org [maintainer] and attach the
> "/usr/ports/textproc/libvisio/work/libvisio-0.0.31/config.log" including the
> output of the failure of your make command. Also, it might be a good idea to
> provide an overview of all packages installed on your system (e.g. a
> /usr/local/sbin/pkg-static info -g -Ea).
> *** Error code 1
> 
> BOOST is installed and has recently been updated:
> 
> pkg info -x boost
> 
> boost-jam-1.55.0
> boost-libs-1.55.0
> boost-python-libs-1.55.0
> 
> 
I cannot reproduce can you send me your config.log?

regards,
Bapt
